mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to contract over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, which compresses corporate margins and triggers negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, driving positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive during periods of industrial production acceleration, or the signal reverses direction in expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in housing starts signals collapsing consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative equity returns in over 65% of cases, or the predictive IC remains below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term market returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to correlate with positive equity returns over rolling 3-year periods, or positive return frequency falls below 50% during expansion quarters.
